topic: Cline vs Roo Code 2026: Best Open-Source VS Code AI Agent Compared

key_points: Comprehensive side-by-side of modes, architecture, and workflow
Roo Code forked from Cline and added multi-mode system (Code, Architect, Ask, Debug)
Cline Plan/Act separation vs Roo multi-agent orchestration
Community stats: Cline 57.9K stars, 4M+ developers; Roo Code 23.8K stars, 1.55M installs
Roo Code more reliable on large multi-file changes
Fewer half-finished edits and less agent thrashing with Roo
Cline better for strict corporate environments needing safety guardrails
Roo Code suited for Solo Founders and SMB CTOs
Three-way comparison including Continue dev
Cline best for human-in-the-loop control
Roo Code best for structured multi-mode workflows
Continue best for lightweight inline assistance
Detailed feature matrix comparison
Boomerang Tasks unique to Roo Code for complex orchestration
Both support BYOK model-agnostic approach
Cline supports JetBrains; Roo Code primarily VS Code
Both ranked in top 5 open-source AI coding tools 2026
Cline ranks higher for community and stability
Roo Code praised for autonomous multi-agent execution
Both are free extensions with BYOK cost model

stats.fact: Cline has 57,900+ GitHub stars and 4 million+ VS Code/JetBrains installations as of May 2026
Roo Code has 23,800+ GitHub stars and 1.55 million VS Code installs as of May 2026
Roo Code has 300+ active contributors as of March 2026
Cline individual developer costs range from $5-$50/month depending on model usage (BYOK)
Cline Teams plan is $20/user/month with 10 free seats available from Q1 2026
OpenHands leads GitHub stars at 68K; Cline second at 58K; Aider third at 41K among open-source coding agents
Roo Code Boomerang Tasks enable parallel sub-agent execution, reducing multi-file task completion time significantly
Both Cline and Roo Code are Apache 2.0 licensed and fully open-source

angles: Roo Code originated as a Cline fork — the fork-vs-origin narrative explaining why devs chose to build Roo instead of contributing to Cline
Boomerang Tasks as a paradigm shift: single-agent vs orchestrated multi-agent workflows inside VS Code
Cost control angle: both are BYOK but model-per-mode assignment in Roo can drastically cut API spend (cheap model for Ask mode, powerful model for Code mode)
Enterprise safety angle: Cline step-by-step approval model vs Roo autonomous execution — regulated industries prefer Cline
JetBrains support gap: Cline supports JetBrains natively; Roo Code has experimental JetBrains bridge but primary support is VS Code only
Custom modes in Roo Code enable domain-specific agents (security auditor mode, data migration mode) that Cline cannot replicate without full prompt engineering
Community velocity: Cline 4M install base vs Roo Code rapid contributor growth (300+ in under 2 years) signals diverging trajectories
Verdict framing: Cline for control, Roo Code for speed — a clean decision framework readers can apply immediately
